steps.instructions: Observe how the device reports battery status. Action: Power on the BladderScan BVI 9400 and check the battery icon or charge indication. Place the device on the charger and observe whether the battery status changes. Allow the unit to remain on charge long enough to determine whether charge level increases. Expected outcome: Battery status should show charging and gradually improve. If the charge level never changes despite verified charger power and proper docking, suspect battery, dock, charging circuit, or internal power issue.

steps.title: 8. Compare With a Known-Good Charger or Dock if Available
steps.instructions: This helps separate scanner failure from charging accessory failure. Action: Place the scanner on a known-good compatible charger or dock if one is available. Alternatively, test a known-good BVI 9400 on the suspect charger if your facility has another compatible unit. Expected outcome: If the problem follows the scanner, suspect battery or internal scanner issue. If the problem stays with the charger or dock, suspect charger, dock, or power supply issue. If a faulty charger is confirmed, remove the charger from service and replace or repair it according to facility process.

steps.title: 9. Check for Battery Age or Usage Pattern
steps.instructions: Poor runtime may be caused by an aging battery rather than an immediate charging failure. Action: Review service history, battery replacement history, and staff reports. Look for patterns such as: Device charges fully but dies quickly. Runtime is poor after normal charging. Battery percentage drops suddenly. Scanner powers off during scan activity. Battery has not been replaced in several years. Expected outcome: Battery condition is assessed based on age, symptoms, and history. If the battery is aged or runtime remains poor after normal charging, remove the scanner from service for battery evaluation or replacement.

steps.title: 10. Perform a Controlled Runtime Check if Safe
steps.instructions: Do this only off-patient and only if it does not delay clinical care. Action: Fully charge the device using a verified charger. Power the device on and monitor whether the battery drains unusually fast during normal standby or test use. Do not use the device clinically during this evaluation if reliability is questionable. Expected outcome: Runtime should be reasonable and stable. If runtime drops quickly or the device shuts off unexpectedly, remove the device from service.

steps.title: 11. Inspect for Heat, Swelling, Odor, or Damage
steps.instructions: Battery-related problems can become safety issues. Action: Check the scanner and charging base for: Unusual heat. Burning smell. Cracked housing. Swollen battery area. Discoloration near charging contacts. Intermittent power when moved. Signs of liquid damage. Expected outcome: No unsafe physical condition is present. If heat, odor, swelling, liquid damage, or physical damage is found, stop troubleshooting immediately, remove the device from service, label it Out of Service, and escalate for repair.

steps.title: 12. Verify Final Device Status
steps.instructions: Before returning the scanner to service, confirm reliable operation. Action: Confirm the scanner powers on normally. Confirm charging indicator behavior is normal. Confirm the device holds charge long enough for expected clinical use. Confirm no power interruptions occur during handling or scanning setup. Expected outcome: The BladderScan BVI 9400 charges properly and operates reliably. If confirmed, return the device to service and document the work order.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Verathon BladderScan BVI 9400 would not hold a charge and powered off shortly after being removed from the charging dock.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet and charger power were good, charging contacts were clean, and the scanner still showed poor runtime after a full charge.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the BladderScan BVI 9400 from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for battery and charging system evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Charging dock or power supply tested
Any unusual heat, odor, swelling, or damage
Whether issue followed scanner or charger
Final device status: returned to service or removed from service

title: Verathon BladderScan BVI 9400 Bladder Scanner - Printer or results documentation failure
description: Troubleshooting failed printouts, missing scan results, poor print quality, or documentation problems after bladder volume measurement.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not delay patient care while troubleshooting a documentation issue. Remove the BladderScan BVI 9400 from patient use if scan results cannot be reliably displayed, printed, or documented. If the scanner is needed for active patient care, use another approved bladder scanner or follow the facility’s alternate assessment process. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without relying on incomplete or missing documentation. Why it matters: A bladder scanner may still scan correctly, but missing or incorrect documentation can affect clinical decisions and charting accuracy. If this resolves the immediate clinical need, stop and document the issue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Documentation Failure
steps.instructions: Confirm exactly what is failing before assuming a printer problem. Ask clinical staff what occurred: No printout produced; Printout is blank; Printout is faded or unreadable; Wrong or missing patient/result information; Results do not save; Scan result disappears before documentation; Staff cannot transfer or record results. Perform a test scan or review a recent stored result if available. Expected outcome: The failure is narrowed to printing, result storage, user workflow, or scanner function. Why it matters: “Printer not working” may actually be a paper issue, battery issue, user workflow issue, or failed scan documentation process.

steps.title: Confirm the Scan Result Is Actually Available
steps.instructions: Verify that the device produced a valid bladder volume result. Power on the unit and check whether scan results appear normally on the display. Confirm that the scan completed and did not show a low-confidence result, error, or canceled scan. Expected outcome: A valid scan result is visible and available for printing or documentation. If no valid result exists, troubleshoot the scan quality or probe issue first and stop.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge and Power Status
steps.instructions: Low battery or unstable power can interfere with printing or result handling. Check the battery level indicator. Place the scanner on its charging cradle or connect it to approved power if applicable. Allow the unit to charge if the battery is low. Attempt printing again after confirming stable power. Expected outcome: The unit has enough power to complete scan documentation and printer operation. If the printer works after charging, return the device to service after confirming normal operation.

steps.title: Inspect Printer Paper Installation
steps.instructions: Check the most common external cause first. Open the printer paper compartment. Confirm that paper is present. Verify that the paper roll is installed in the correct direction for thermal printing. Make sure the paper feeds freely and is not jammed, folded, wrinkled, or misrouted. Expected outcome: Paper is loaded correctly and can advance normally. If correcting the paper roll restores printing, stop and document the resolution.

steps.title: Check for Paper Jam or Debris
steps.instructions: A small paper obstruction can prevent printing or cause partial printouts. Inspect the paper path. Remove any torn paper pieces or loose debris. Do not use sharp tools that could damage the printer mechanism. Close the paper compartment securely and attempt another print. Expected outcome: Paper feeds smoothly without binding. If the jam is cleared and printouts are normal, return the unit to service.

steps.title: Verify Thermal Paper Condition
steps.instructions: Blank or faded printouts may be caused by incorrect or degraded paper. Confirm that approved thermal paper is being used. Check if the paper appears old, glossy on the wrong side, heat damaged, or stored improperly. Replace the roll with known-good thermal paper. Attempt another print. Expected outcome: Printout becomes readable with proper thermal paper. If print quality improves, document the paper replacement and return the device to service.

steps.title: Check Printer Door or Cover Closure
steps.instructions: The printer may not operate if the cover is not fully latched. Open and reseat the printer cover. Confirm that the cover clicks or seats fully closed. Attempt to print again. Expected outcome: The printer recognizes the paper compartment as closed and prints normally. If reseating the cover resolves the issue, stop and document the finding.

steps.title: Confirm Staff Printing Workflow
steps.instructions: Make sure the failure is not caused by a missed menu step or timing issue. Review how staff are attempting to print or document the result. Confirm that the result screen is active and that the print option is selected correctly. Check whether staff are clearing, rescanning, or powering off the unit before documentation is completed. Expected outcome: The scanner is being used in the correct sequence for result documentation. If workflow correction resolves the problem, provide user guidance and document the education given.

steps.title: Check Date, Time, and Result Information
steps.instructions: Documentation may be considered failed if printouts are missing correct identifiers or timestamps. Check whether date and time appear correct on printed or displayed results. Verify whether patient ID entry, if used at the facility, is being entered correctly. Confirm that the device settings match facility expectations for result documentation. Expected outcome: Printed results contain usable scan information for clinical documentation. If settings or workflow correction resolves the issue, document what was corrected.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Retest
steps.instructions: A temporary software or printer communication issue may clear with a controlled restart. Power the BladderScan BVI 9400 off. Wait briefly. Power the unit back on. Perform or retrieve a test result and attempt printing again. Expected outcome: The printer and result documentation function normally after restart. If normal operation returns, monitor for recurrence and document the successful retest.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Printer or documentation failures may follow drops, fluid exposure, or rough handling. Inspect the scanner housing, printer cover, paper door, keypad/buttons, display, charging contacts, and probe connection. Look for: Cracked housing; Loose printer door; Missing latch; Signs of fluid ingress; Unusual smell; Heat damage; Evidence of impact. Expected outcome: No visible damage is present that would explain printer or system malfunction. If damage is found, remove the device from service and send it for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Test With Known-Good Consumables and Accessories
steps.instructions: Rule out simple external causes before escalating. Install a known-good paper roll. Confirm the device has adequate battery charge. Use the correct scanner, cradle, and accessories assigned to the unit. Attempt another print or documentation test. Expected outcome: The device prints or documents correctly with known-good external items. If the issue remains after known-good paper and power are verified, escalation is appropriate.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Verathon BladderScan BVI 9400 would scan but would not print the bladder volume result.
CCR.Cause: Troubleshooting found the thermal paper roll was installed backward, causing blank printouts.
CCR.Resolution: Reinstalled the paper roll correctly, performed a test scan printout, verified readable results, and returned the scanner to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether the scan result displayed normally
Whether the issue was no print, blank print, faded print, or missing documentation
Whether known-good thermal paper was tested
Any paper jam or debris found
Whether date/time and result information printed correctly
Any visible damage, fluid exposure, heat, or unusual smell

title: Verathon BladderScan BVI 9400 Bladder Scanner - Calibration, self-test, or startup failure
description: Troubleshooting BladderScan BVI 9400 startup, self-test, or calibration problems caused by power, probe, battery, cradle, or user-accessible setup issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot the BladderScan BVI 9400 while it is being used for active patient assessment. Remove the scanner from patient use if it will not start, fails self-test, displays calibration-related errors, or gives unreliable scan behavior. Use another bladder scanner or approved clinical assessment method if bladder volume measurement is needed. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed or based on an unreliable device. Why it matters: A bladder scanner that fails startup, self-test, or calibration checks may produce inaccurate or unavailable measurements.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Failure
steps.instructions: Confirm exactly what the device is doing before assuming a hardware failure. Power the unit on and observe the startup sequence. Check whether the issue is unit will not power on. Check whether the issue is startup freezes. Check whether the issue is self-test fails. Check whether the issue is calibration message appears. Check whether the issue is probe is not recognized. Check whether the issue is screen is blank or dim. Check whether the issue is device powers off unexpectedly. Check whether the issue is error returns after restart. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified and documented. If the unit starts normally after restart and passes checks, return it to service only after confirming normal scan operation.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge and Battery Seating
steps.instructions: Low or unstable battery power can cause startup and self-test problems. Confirm the battery is properly installed and seated. Place the unit on the charging base or connect it to the approved charging setup. Allow the battery to charge if the charge level is low. Check for charging indicators on the unit or charger. Expected outcome: The unit powers on normally and completes startup with adequate battery power. If the issue resolves after charging or reseating the battery, document the finding and stop.

steps.title: Inspect the Charging Base and Power Supply
steps.instructions: A charging or power issue may appear as a scanner failure. Verify the charger is plugged into a known-good outlet. Inspect the power adapter, cord, and charger contacts for damage, looseness, bent pins, fluid residue, or corrosion. Test the outlet with another device or outlet tester if appropriate. If available, try a known-good compatible charger or charging base. Expected outcome: The scanner charges consistently and powers on normally. If the unit only fails with one charger or outlet, remove the faulty accessory from service.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe and Cable Connection
steps.instructions: The BVI 9400 depends on the probe and cable connection for proper startup and functional checks. Inspect the probe, cable, connector, and strain relief for visible damage. Look for bent pins, cracked housings, dried gel, fluid intrusion, or loose connections. Confirm the probe connector is fully seated. Disconnect and reconnect the probe if the design allows normal user-level reconnection. Expected outcome: The device recognizes the probe and completes startup or self-test. If the probe or cable is damaged, remove the device or accessory from service and send it for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Check for Contamination or Fluid Intrusion
steps.instructions: Fluid residue can interfere with buttons, contacts, charging, or probe communication. Inspect the scanner body, probe, charger contacts, and battery area for cleaning fluid, gel buildup, residue, corrosion, or signs of liquid entry. Clean only according to approved facility and manufacturer cleaning practices. Allow the device to dry fully before retesting. Expected outcome: External contamination is removed and the unit starts normally. If fluid intrusion, corrosion, or unusual residue is found inside user-accessible areas, stop troubleshooting and escalate.

steps.title: Check Display, Buttons, and Keypad Response
steps.instructions: Startup may appear failed if the display or buttons are not responding correctly. Confirm the power button responds normally. Check whether buttons feel stuck, damaged, unusually soft, or non-responsive. Observe whether the screen backlight turns on, flickers, or remains blank. Expected outcome: Controls respond normally and the display shows the startup screen. If buttons are stuck or the display remains blank despite confirmed power, remove the unit from service.

steps.title: Restart the Device
steps.instructions: A temporary software lockup may clear with a proper restart. Power the unit off. Wait at least 30 seconds. Power the unit back on. Observe whether startup and self-test complete normally. Expected outcome: The device boots normally and no self-test or calibration error returns. If the failure returns repeatedly, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Confirm the Device Is Not Being Used Outside Normal Conditions
steps.instructions: Environmental or handling issues can contribute to startup or calibration failures. Confirm the scanner is dry, clean, and at normal room temperature. Do not test immediately after the device has been stored in a very cold, hot, or humid area. Allow the unit to acclimate if it was recently transported or stored outside the clinical area. Expected outcome: The device starts and self-checks normally under normal operating conditions. If the problem resolves after acclimation and drying, document the condition found.

steps.title: Attempt User-Level Calibration Only If Approved
steps.instructions: Do not perform service-level calibration steps unless your department is trained, authorized, and has the required tools. Check whether the issue requires routine user-accessible calibration or a calibration verification process. Use only approved calibration accessories, targets, or procedures available to Clinical Engineering. Do not bypass calibration warnings or clear errors without correcting the cause. Expected outcome: The scanner completes the approved calibration or verification process successfully. If calibration fails again, remove the unit from service.

steps.title: Check for Repeated or Intermittent Startup Failure
steps.instructions: Intermittent failures should be treated seriously even if the device occasionally starts. Power cycle the device several times. Confirm whether the failure happens every time or only intermittently. Check whether the problem changes when the probe cable, charger connection, or battery is gently repositioned. Expected outcome: The device starts consistently without cable movement, power interruption, or repeated errors. If startup depends on cable position, battery movement, or repeated attempts, the device should not be returned to service.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Verathon BladderScan BVI 9400 would not complete startup and displayed a calibration or self-test failure before scanning.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found the scanner battery charged and the outlet tested good, but the device continued to fail startup/self-test after probe reconnection and restart.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the BladderScan BVI 9400 from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation/vendor repair due to persistent startup/self-test failure.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior, including any calibration or self-test message displayed
Accessories swapped, including charging base, power supply, battery, probe, or cable

Power behavior, including outlet tested and confirmed good, battery charge level or charging status, charging base indicator behavior, power supply condition, and whether the failure occurs at startup or during scan setup
Environmental factors, including unusual heat, smell, fluid residue, corrosion, storage temperature, humidity, or whether the issue is constant or intermittent
Indicator lights, including charging base indicator behavior, display behavior, and button/keypad response
Final device status, including whether the device was returned to service, removed from service, labeled Out of Service, or sent for repair evaluation
